answer: \boxed{Evariste Gamelin wields his power on the Revolutionary Tribunal to execute not only political enemies of the Revolution but also his personal acquaintances—including loved ones—driven by a thirst for vengeance and ideological zeal. He justifies his actions as a fight against counter-revolutionary plots, yet his decisions are increasingly motivated by hatred toward those who oppose his Jacobin extremism. Ultimately, his obsession with justice and terror leads to his own downfall, as he becomes both judge and victim of the guillotine.
